Create a o2f theme

Source: R/themes.R
o2f_theme.Rd

Construct a theme object that controls the appearance of tables generated by o2f().

Usage

o2f_theme(
  name = "custom",
  scolor = "blue!10",
  header_bold = TRUE,
  header_color = NULL,
  font_size = NULL,
  font_family = NULL,
  document_class = "article",
  extra_packages = NULL,
  booktabs = TRUE,
  striped = TRUE
)

Arguments

name

Character string. Display name for the theme.

scolor

LaTeX color specification for alternating row shading (e.g., "blue!10"). Set to "white" or use striped=FALSE to disable.

header_bold

Logical. If TRUE, table headers are rendered in bold.

header_color

LaTeX color for header background. NULL for no background.

font_size

LaTeX font size command (e.g., "footnotesize", "small").

font_family

Optional LaTeX font family name. NULL keeps the document's default font. NULL for default size.

document_class

LaTeX document class. Defaults to "article".

extra_packages

List of additional LaTeX package specifications.

booktabs

Logical. If TRUE, uses booktabs package for professional horizontal rules.

striped

Logical. If TRUE, applies alternating row colors.

Value

A o2f_theme object (list with class "o2f_theme").

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
my_theme <- o2f_theme(
  name = "custom",
  scolor = "green!8",
  header_bold = TRUE,
  font_size = "small"
)
o2f_theme_set(my_theme)
o2f(mtcars)
} # }
